key_insight: A hybrid model uses a sharp-interface thermal solver and a diffuse-interface flow solver to better capture laser-induced melt pool dynamics with rapid evaporation.
engineering_relevance: This enables more accurate prediction of recoil pressure, surface tension-driven flow, and melt pool behavior in laser welding and laser powder bed fusion.
method: Sharp-interface CutFEM for accurate heat transfer and temperature gradients
Level-set one-fluid diffuse-interface FEM for multiphase flow with large density ratios
Couples both by extending temperature to the interface region for force evaluation
evidence_signal: The abstract reports second-order spatial convergence for the thermal model and about one order-of-magnitude better accuracy than a purely diffuse-interface model on the same mesh.
watch_item: The coupling depends on a consistent temperature extension near the interface, so implementation details may strongly affect stability and force accuracy.
paper_title: A hybrid sharp-diffuse interface approach to accurately model melt pool dynamics with rapid evaporation in laser-based processing of metals
